fluid_biomarker_papers.label: Fluid biomarker papers
fluid_biomarker_papers.definition: Human or other living-organism studies that collect biofluids and evaluate biomarker utility for clinically relevant or pre-clinically informative purposes.
must_address: study registration and analytic-plan disclosure
power and planned versus actual sample size
participant accounting for screened, collected, analyzed, quality-passed, and clinically assessed samples
blinding of biomarker measurement, handling, quality control, and analysis roles
sample timing, collection state, handling conditions, storage, batch assignment, lot bridging, and technical failures
assay validation, replicates, coefficient of variation, lower and upper limits, dilutional linearity, specificity, and spike-recovery
validity and reliability of the clinically relevant assessments paired to the biomarker
statistical assumptions, outlier handling, missing-data handling, effect sizes, confidence intervals, and multiple-comparison management
replication or external validation status
data availability, analytic code availability, biofluid sample availability, and open/public availability plan

pathophysiological_mechanistic_studies.label: Pathophysiological mechanistic studies
pathophysiological_mechanistic_studies.definition: Systematic, controlled in vivo or in vitro experimental manipulations intended to advance fundamental mechanistic knowledge relevant to traumatic brain or spinal cord injury.
must_address: study registration and analytic-plan disclosure
power and planned versus actual sample size
subject accounting for inclusion, manipulation, analysis, interpretability, deaths, and incomplete assessments
randomization method and adequacy assessment
blinding of manipulations, materials handling, analyses, and labeling
experimental-manipulation timing, state, devices, storage, batching, technical failures, and unexpected events
target engagement, dose response, cross validation, specificity controls, lesion or stimulation verification, and positive/negative controls
primary-analysis accuracy, assay characteristics, statistical assumptions, outlier handling, missing-data handling, and multiple-comparison management
replication or external validation status
data availability, analytic code availability, sample availability, and open/public availability plan
critical_for_readiness: randomization or explicit rationale for non-randomization

snapshot.notes: Manual capture from the official Journal of Neurotrauma instructions plus the supplied April 2026 article-type-specific transparency statements for fluid biomarker papers and pathophysiological mechanistic studies. This journal explicitly considers original research, reviews, short communications, and letters to the editor; case reports are not accepted. It accepts format-neutral first submissions but still requires IRB/disclosure elements and a 500-word Transparency, Rigor, and Reproducibility Summary after the conclusion. Operational fit rule: mechanistic biomarker and pathophysiological studies can remain bounded in translational maturity if claims are explicitly limited and the evidence chain, declarations, article-type-specific transparency checklist, and reproducibility summary are complete.

target_context.detail: Prioritize an OCLN-centered barrier-repair packet first because the current BBB target packets are strongest around OCLN, CLDN5, and TJP1, and this lane already anchors the BBB-to-immune-infiltration bridge. Direct corpus signal: The treatment repaired BBB structure and function.

genomics_context.detail: Current genomics support is bounded and indirect: transcriptomic or single-cell-like inflammatory signals exist in the repo, but there is no clean endotype-specific 10x packet yet.

snapshot.notes: Official Oxford instructions list feature articles, review articles, case reports, brief reports, commentaries, and letters to the editor. This is a realistic military-facing target for operational TBI, biomarker, and system papers that can stay clinically grounded without overclaiming translational actionability.

target_context.detail: Use NLRP3 as the primary perturbation node because it is the strongest inflammatory target packet in the repo, links directly to the mito-to-inflammasome transition, and gives a cleaner perturbation frame than chasing one downstream cytokine at a time. Direct corpus signal: Extracellular ATP (eATP) exhibits dual roles in TBI: high concentrations activate P2X7R, leading to NLRP3 inflammasome activation and pyroptosis, while low concentrations activate SUR1–TRPM4–AQP4 complexes, contributing to cerebral edema.

trial_context.detail: no trial attachment surfaced in this build

genomics_context.detail: Current genomics support is bounded and indirect: transcriptomic or single-cell-like inflammatory signals exist in the repo, but there is no clean endotype-specific 10x packet yet.

task_ledger.label: Resolve the lead contradiction or evidence tension
task_ledger.rationale: The current lane still carries a material blocker: The packet needs more direct cohort-level translation into chronic outcomes and transcriptomic stratifiers.
task_ledger.success_signal: A future pass reduces or removes the contradiction/blocker language from the candidate ledger.
task_ledger.execution_note: For review framing, the contradiction stack is carried explicitly as bounded limitation and future-work language rather than requiring experimental resolution before packaging. Lead issue: The packet needs more direct cohort-level translation into chronic outcomes and transcriptomic stratifiers.

fluid_biomarker_papers.label: Fluid biomarker papers
fluid_biomarker_papers.definition: Human or other living-organism studies that collect biofluids and evaluate biomarker utility for clinically relevant or pre-clinically informative purposes.
must_address: study registration and analytic-plan disclosure
power and planned versus actual sample size
participant accounting for screened, collected, analyzed, quality-passed, and clinically assessed samples
blinding of biomarker measurement, handling, quality control, and analysis roles
sample timing, collection state, handling conditions, storage, batch assignment, lot bridging, and technical failures
assay validation, replicates, coefficient of variation, lower and upper limits, dilutional linearity, specificity, and spike-recovery
validity and reliability of the clinically relevant assessments paired to the biomarker
statistical assumptions, outlier handling, missing-data handling, effect sizes, confidence intervals, and multiple-comparison management
replication or external validation status
data availability, analytic code availability, biofluid sample availability, and open/public availability plan

pathophysiological_mechanistic_studies.label: Pathophysiological mechanistic studies
pathophysiological_mechanistic_studies.definition: Systematic, controlled in vivo or in vitro experimental manipulations intended to advance fundamental mechanistic knowledge relevant to traumatic brain or spinal cord injury.
must_address: study registration and analytic-plan disclosure
power and planned versus actual sample size
subject accounting for inclusion, manipulation, analysis, interpretability, deaths, and incomplete assessments
randomization method and adequacy assessment
blinding of manipulations, materials handling, analyses, and labeling
experimental-manipulation timing, state, devices, storage, batching, technical failures, and unexpected events
target engagement, dose response, cross validation, specificity controls, lesion or stimulation verification, and positive/negative controls
primary-analysis accuracy, assay characteristics, statistical assumptions, outlier handling, missing-data handling, and multiple-comparison management
replication or external validation status
data availability, analytic code availability, sample availability, and open/public availability plan
critical_for_readiness: randomization or explicit rationale for non-randomization

snapshot.notes: Manual capture from the official Journal of Neurotrauma instructions plus the supplied April 2026 article-type-specific transparency statements for fluid biomarker papers and pathophysiological mechanistic studies. This journal explicitly considers original research, reviews, short communications, and letters to the editor; case reports are not accepted. It accepts format-neutral first submissions but still requires IRB/disclosure elements and a 500-word Transparency, Rigor, and Reproducibility Summary after the conclusion. Operational fit rule: mechanistic biomarker and pathophysiological studies can remain bounded in translational maturity if claims are explicitly limited and the evidence chain, declarations, article-type-specific transparency checklist, and reproducibility summary are complete.

snapshot.notes: Official Oxford instructions list feature articles, review articles, case reports, brief reports, commentaries, and letters to the editor. This is a realistic military-facing target for operational TBI, biomarker, and system papers that can stay clinically grounded without overclaiming translational actionability.

target_context.detail: Use a PRKN-first perturbation packet because Parkin overexpression is already tied in this corpus to reduced mitochondrial dysfunction, apoptosis, and neurotoxicity, while CYBB remains the main challenger for ROS-driven inflammatory amplification. Direct corpus signal: UC-MSC-derived exosomes reduce neuronal apoptosis via PINK1/Parkin-mediated mitophagy.

trial_context.detail: no trial attachment surfaced in this build

genomics_context.detail: A chronic mild genomics signal is partially supported by RNA-seq and single-cell-like TBI resources in the repo, but no mature 10x endotype export has been attached yet.

task_ledger.label: Resolve the lead contradiction or evidence tension

task_ledger.execution_note: For review framing, the contradiction stack is carried explicitly as bounded limitation and future-work language rather than requiring experimental resolution before packaging. Lead issue: Study found no significant correlation, contradicting the hypothesis of cumulative damage.

target_context.detail: Use a DYRK1A-first packet because the current TBI corpus already contains a direct perturbation anchor in which DYRK1A inhibition with SM07883 reduced tau burden and improved recovery, while NLRP3 and AQP4 remain important upstream challengers rather than the first tau-lane perturbation. Direct corpus signal: SM07883 treatment reduces RHI-induced tau hyperphosphorylation at Thr231 and Ser202 in the brainstem.

Journal of Neurotrauma Transparency, Rigor and Reproducibility Statement for FLUID BIOMARKERS Papers (01/05/22)

fluid_biomarker_papers.label: Fluid biomarker papers
fluid_biomarker_papers.definition: Human or other living-organism studies that collect biofluids and evaluate biomarker utility for clinically relevant or pre-clinically informative purposes.
must_address: study registration and analytic-plan disclosure
power and planned versus actual sample size
participant accounting for screened, collected, analyzed, quality-passed, and clinically assessed samples
blinding of biomarker measurement, handling, quality control, and analysis roles
sample timing, collection state, handling conditions, storage, batch assignment, lot bridging, and technical failures
assay validation, replicates, coefficient of variation, lower and upper limits, dilutional linearity, specificity, and spike-recovery
validity and reliability of the clinically relevant assessments paired to the biomarker
statistical assumptions, outlier handling, missing-data handling, effect sizes, confidence intervals, and multiple-comparison management
replication or external validation status
data availability, analytic code availability, biofluid sample availability, and open/public availability plan

pathophysiological_mechanistic_studies.label: Pathophysiological mechanistic studies
pathophysiological_mechanistic_studies.definition: Systematic, controlled in vivo or in vitro experimental manipulations intended to advance fundamental mechanistic knowledge relevant to traumatic brain or spinal cord injury.
must_address: study registration and analytic-plan disclosure
power and planned versus actual sample size
subject accounting for inclusion, manipulation, analysis, interpretability, deaths, and incomplete assessments
randomization method and adequacy assessment
blinding of manipulations, materials handling, analyses, and labeling
experimental-manipulation timing, state, devices, storage, batching, technical failures, and unexpected events
target engagement, dose response, cross validation, specificity controls, lesion or stimulation verification, and positive/negative controls
primary-analysis accuracy, assay characteristics, statistical assumptions, outlier handling, missing-data handling, and multiple-comparison management
replication or external validation status
data availability, analytic code availability, sample availability, and open/public availability plan
critical_for_readiness: randomization or explicit rationale for non-randomization
